symptom: a knock to the head in a match then an hour of seeming totally fine before going downhill fast
hook: there was a lens shaped pocket of blood pressing the brain away from the skull
whatYouSee: A lens shaped collection of blood bulges inward with a sharply defined convex edge that does not cross the seams of the skull.
whyItMatters: A skull fracture has torn an artery running just under the bone and the bleeding is fast because it comes from an artery not a vein.
treatment: This needs emergency surgical drainage before the rapidly expanding blood crushes the brain.
takeaway: A lens shaped bleed after a clear headed interval is the classic epidural pattern and it can turn fatal within hours.
laypersonQuestion: Does that shape look like a lens to you?
seedHint: Look at the shape of the pocket and whether it bulges in or hugs the curve.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in after a knock to the head in a match then an hour of seeming totally fine before going downhill fast.

Then the CT scan loaded 😭

And there was a lens shaped pocket of blood pressing the brain away from the skull.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Epidural hematoma

👀 What you see:
A lens shaped collection of blood bulges inward with a sharply defined convex edge that does not cross the seams of the skull.

🦴 Why it matters:
A skull fracture has torn an artery running just under the bone and the bleeding is fast because it comes from an artery not a vein.

💊 Treatment:
This needs emergency surgical drainage before the rapidly expanding blood crushes the brain.
